name: El Semillero Lagoons Loop
name_original: Castrogonzalo — circular por Lagunas de El Semillero
description: Hike the moderate 4.3-mile (6.9 km) Castrogonzalo circular route, exploring the scenic El Semillero Lagoons and Esla River banks.
region_intro: Hiking around Castrogonzalo offers diverse natural landscapes and historical attractions within the province of Zamora. The region is characterized by the Esla River, extensive greenways like the Vía Verde de la Plata, and scenic areas such as the El Semillero Lagoons. Hikers can explore "pseudo-steppes" and riverine environments, which contribute to a rich diversity of flora and fauna. The area also features archaeological vestiges of a medieval castle and a 13th-century bridge, providing historical context to many routes. Best hiking trails around Castrogonzalo The most popular hiking route is Vía de la Plata and Vía Verde del Oeste Loop, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This route combines two significant greenways, offering varied scenery along disused railway lines. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Semillero Lagoons Loop, a moderate 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This trail leads through the scenic El Semillero Lagoons, providing a tranquil natural experience. Local hikers also love the Vía Verde de la Plata from Barcial del Barco — circular by the río Esla, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and along the Vía Verde de la Plata, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Castrogonzalo is defined by riverine environments, extensive greenways, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Castrogonzalo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Castrogonzalo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Castrogonzalo?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around Castrogonzalo, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The komoot community has explored these routes more than 40 times.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Castrogonzalo?
A: Hiking in Castrogonzalo offers diverse landscapes, including picturesque riverbank environments along the Esla River, extensive greenways like the Vía Verde de la Plata, and scenic areas such as the El Semillero Lagoons. You can also find unique "pseudo-steppes" towards Fuentes de Ropel.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Castrogonzalo?
A: Yes, Castrogonzalo features 8 easy hiking trails that are well-suited for beginners or families. These routes often follow the gentle gradients of the greenways, providing accessible and enjoyable outdoor experiences.

Q: Are there historical sites or landmarks to explore on the trails?
A: Many routes in Castrogonzalo incorporate historical elements. You can find archaeological vestiges of a medieval castle and a 13th-century medieval bridge. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Azoague Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, all of which are popular spots for walkers.

Q: Can I see any wildlife while hiking in Castrogonzalo?
A: Yes, the region offers opportunities for wildlife observation. The "pseudo-steppes" around Castrogonzalo, particularly towards Fuentes de Ropel, are an ideal habitat for observing Great Bustards (avutardas). The diverse flora and fauna near the Esla River also enhance the natural appeal for enthusiasts.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Castrogonzalo?
A: Several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305470296/el-semillero-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Semillero Lagoons Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.3-mile path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305437921/via-verde-de-la-plata-from-barcial-del-barco-circular-by-the-rio-esla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Plata from Barcial del Barco — circular by the río Esla&lt;/a&gt; offers a 5.6-mile experience through riverine landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Castrogonzalo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil natural experiences, the well-maintained greenways, and the blend of natural beauty with historical landmarks.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Castrogonzalo?
A: Most outdoor trails in Castrogonzalo, especially the greenways and river paths, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: Are there any routes that combine different greenways?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305317182/via-de-la-plata-and-via-verde-del-oeste-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata and Vía Verde del Oeste Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that combines two significant greenways, offering varied scenery along disused railway lines.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Castrogonzalo?
A: The spring and autumn months typically offer the most pleasant weather for hiking in Castrogonzalo, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter offers a quieter experience, though some paths might be damp.

Q: Are there any trails near Benavente that are easily accessible from Castrogonzalo?
A: Yes, Castrogonzalo is close to Benavente, and you can find trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31849402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castillo de Benavente – Old Train Station of Benavente loop from Benavente&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Esla River loop from Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, which are easily reachable and offer additional exploration opportunities.
tips: Hiking trails around the nature reserve and fishing ponds on the banks of the Esla River on the outskirts of the town of Castrogonzalo.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Castrogonzalo — circular by Lagunas de El Semillero trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied terrain. You'll encounter about 3.86 kilometers of general paths or trails ("senda"), alongside stretches of road ("carretera" and "carretera nacional") totaling around 2 kilometers. Shorter sections include specific footpaths ("sendero" and "sendero de montaña"), and urban streets ("calle"). Expect a mix of paved tracks, natural paths, asphalt, and unpaved tracks.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route winds through picturesque natural surroundings, including a natural reserve and fishing ponds. A primary highlight is the journey along the tranquil El Semillero Lagoons, on the banks of the Esla River. You'll enjoy scenic views of the river and the lagoons, with opportunities for birdwatching, especially migratory species when the lagoons are full.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, classified as a moderate hike, its relatively low elevation gain and manageable length make it generally suitable for individuals with good physical condition. It's also described as being great for any fitness level with mostly accessible paths, making it a good option for families.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Castrogonzalo — circular by Lagunas de El Semillero is a circular route, offering a continuous experience of the area's natural beauty without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout the year, but spring and autumn are often ideal for comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery. Visiting when the lagoons are full can enhance birdwatching opportunities.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural reserve areas, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Castrogonzalo — circular by Lagunas de El Semillero trail or access the Lagunas de El Semillero area.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, as a circular route starting and ending in Castrogonzalo, you would typically find parking within the town or near the access points to the lagoons. Look for public parking spaces in Castrogonzalo.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 35% of this route follows sections of the Camino de Santiago del Sureste.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options nearby?
faqs.answer: Castrogonzalo, being the starting and ending point of the circular route, offers local amenities. You can expect to find cafes, pubs, and potentially accommodation options within the town for refreshments or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's location within a natural reserve and along the Esla River and El Semillero Lagoons, you have a good chance of observing local flora and fauna. Birdwatching is a particular highlight, especially for migratory birds when the lagoons are full.
